name: verification-agent
description: A three-step agent that post-processes any LLM chatbot response to identify domain-knowledge claims, shortlist the 3 most verifiable ones, and find accurate literature citations for each. Graph-structural observations are automatically excluded since they are grounded in exact computation.
author: Automorph

capabilities.name: Mark Claims
capabilities.description: Reads every sentence in an LLM response and labels each as a verifiable domain-knowledge claim or not. Graph-structural observations (orbits, group order, node IDs) are never marked.

responseText.description: Full LLM response text to analyse

capabilities.name: Find Citations
capabilities.description: Receives a list of pre-selected claims (isClaim: true) and finds a specific, real published source for each. Claims with no verifiable source are de-marked. Does not fabricate references.

marked.description: Output of mark-claims, optionally filtered to 3 shortlisted candidates

citation.description: FirstAuthor et al., Title, Venue, Year — null if no real source found

environment.name: OLLAMA_API_KEY
environment.description: API key for the Ollama cloud endpoint (nemotron-3-super:cloud model)

environment.name: PORT
environment.description: Port the standalone agent server listens on

integration.usage: new VerificationAgent({ container: el, apiBase: 'https://your-agent-url', domain: 'finance_research' })
steps: 2. agent.shortlist()        — Step 2: client-side, pick top 3 by specificity
3. agent.cite()             — Step 3: find citations for the 3 shortlisted claims
